narration.title: Clawrium GUI walkthrough
narration.text: Welcome to Clawrium — a quick tour of every tab in the clawctl GUI.

narration.title: Dashboard
narration.text: The Dashboard is your fleet overview — total agents, how many are running, providers configured, and a seven-day token-usage chart.

narration.title: Agents
narration.text: The Agents tab lists every agent installed across every host in your fleet, with its current status, type, and provider.

narration.title: Topology
narration.text: Topology shows the physical layout of your fleet — every host you've connected, and which agents live where.

narration.title: Providers
narration.text: Providers are the AI backends powering your agents — Anthropic, OpenAI, Bedrock, OpenRouter, and others, each with their default models.

narration.title: Skills
narration.text: Skills are reusable behavior bundles. You can install them onto any agent — test-driven workflows, code review, release announcements, and more.

narration.title: Integrations
narration.text: Integrations connect your fleet to external channels like Slack and Discord, so agents can post updates or receive commands.

narration.title: Settings
narration.text: Settings is where global preferences live — your config directory, token-tracking storage, GUI port, and a danger zone for resets.

narration.title: Clawrium GUI walkthrough
narration.text: That's every tab. Spin up the GUI yourself with clawctl gui, and head to the docs to get started.
